_status_note: verified_in_runner = isModify flag in _earlyCheckReady confirmed in source AND mirrored unit test passes. Unit test directly exercises the isModify exclusion path using fixture data.
title: Modify booking: reschedule to conflicting time — alternatives shown, old booking not overwritten
failing_behavior: When customer tried to reschedule to a time already taken, the system either (a) wrote the booking over the existing one causing double-booking, or (b) the customer's own existing booking was flagged as a conflict against themselves, blocking the reschedule entirely.
root_cause: _earlyCheckReady did not pass isModify to the availability check. The customer's own confirmed booking was NOT excluded from conflict detection, causing a false customer_conflict on a slot the customer is trying to VACATE.

conversation.content: Can I reschedule my appointment to 10am instead?

Let me check 10 AM for Helen...
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Gel Manicure"],"staff":"Helen","date":"2026-04-14","time":"10:00","name":"Jane","phone":"4085551234","lang":"en","pendingAction":"modify_booking","existingBookingId":null}]

precondition: Another customer already has Helen booked at 10:00 AM on 2026-04-14.
expected_behavior: avail.valid=false with key='conflict'. Alternatives suggested. Old booking NOT overwritten. No false customer_conflict from the customer's own booking.
runner_coverage: Availability logic test 'CASE-006 regression: customer can reschedule to slot their own booking occupied' directly exercises this path.
